svg.title: Iterative correction (ICE) of a Hi-C contact matrix
svg.desc: A four-panel figure showing iterative matrix balancing on the same Hi-C window. Panel A is the raw contact matrix at 10 kb resolution showing two bright vertical bands and one dark band indicating bin-specific coverage bias, plus background TAD and compartment structure mostly hidden. Panel B is after one ICE iteration, with the bands still visible but less extreme. Panel C is after iteration 20, with the bands gone and triangular TAD blocks along the diagonal and a faint compartment checkerboard now visible. Panel D is a convergence plot showing the standard deviation of row sums decaying exponentially against iteration count from about 1.0 to near zero over twenty iterations.

g.text: Drive row and column sums toward a uniform target; banding bias dissolves; structure remains.
first pass tames the worst bands
banding gone; TAD blocks now visible on the diagonal
σ(row sums) decays exponentially; "balanced" by iteration 20.
Each iteration: row-sum, rescale, repeat. Converges in 20 iterations; KR balancing (Knight–Ruiz, 2013) reaches the same fixed point in fewer steps via a Newton update.
